task_summary: Add a bounded deterministic presence loop to mew-ghost, including idle/attentive/coding/waiting/blocked classification, stable refresh snapshots, README refresh contract, CLI/output proof, and hermetic tests.
tests_run: UV_CACHE_DIR=.uv-cache uv run python experiments/mew-ghost/ghost.py --format state --refresh-count 2
UV_CACHE_DIR=.uv-cache uv run python experiments/mew-ghost/ghost.py --format state --output /tmp/mew-ghost-sp14-state.json --refresh-count 2
UV_CACHE_DIR=.uv-cache uv run python experiments/mew-ghost/ghost.py --format state --live-active-window --refresh-count 1
notes: SP14 was implemented by mew in session #27 with --model gpt-5.5, --auth /Users/mk/.codex/auth.json, and write scope limited to experiments/mew-ghost. Before work, origin/main was repeatedly pulled/rebased from the current repo root as required. The first implementation draft failed before edits because the model emitted invalid JSON while preparing a large batch. Mew then proposed a source-only patch #320; auto-approval failed the focused verifier because tests and README were not updated with the source. Operator rejected #320 and steered toward one complete source+tests+README batch. Mew proposed complete batch #325-#327; verifier failed because a live Safari notes case was classified as coding instead of attentive. Mew repaired with corrected batch #333-#335; auto-approval #336-#338 applied the corrected files and the focused verifier passed 15 tests. Operator rejected stale approvals #325-#327 after the corrected batch had already applied and commented on existing issue #10 because the stale failed approval pattern repeated. No Codex product-code rescue edits were made. Real live macOS Accessibility success remains intentionally unrequired; live opt-in behavior is covered by injected providers/runners plus a manual structured fallback command.
